2606.23604.title: Polycepta: Object-Centric Appearance Estimation for Multi-Object Tracking
2606.23604.paper_summary: The tracking-by-detection paradigm in multi-object tracking (MOT) typically relies on static appearance descriptors to complement motion estimation. However, these descriptors are frame-independent, limiting their robustness as visual cues. Since such descriptors are often obtained from computationally intensive pretrained backbones, real-time MOT systems frequently abandon appearance cues altogether and rely solely on motion prediction and geometric association. In this work, we introduce Polycepta, an object-centric appearance state estimation framework that reformulates appearance modeling as a recursive estimation problem rather than a frame-wise matching task. Polycepta constructs and continuously updates an independent appearance state for each tracked object, enabling future appearance representations to be estimated from accumulated observations. Polycepta is encouraged to learn the appearance-state construction of object-specific representations rather than memorize them through a proposed learning strategy, enabling appearance estimation for unseen classes. A key property of Polycepta is that the quality of appearance estimation improves as object states evolve during inference. While conventional appearance descriptors remain static or degrade over time, Polycepta progressively refines appearance estimates as additional observations are accumulated. Extensive experiments on KITTI, the Waymo Open Dataset, and MOT17 demonstrate consistent reductions in identity switches and improvements in tracking performance when integrated into the tracking-by-detection pipelines. Polycepta operates at 90.57 Hz and delivers state-of-the-art performance on the KITTI benchmark when integrated into the RobMOT framework, achieving a MOTA of 92.27\%.
2606.23604.paper_summary_zh: 多物體追蹤（MOT）中的追蹤-通過-檢測範式通常依賴靜態外觀描述符來輔助運動估計。<lb/>然而，這些描述符是幀無關的，限制了它們作為視覺線索的穩健性。<lb/>由於這些描述符通常來自計算密集型的預訓練骨幹，因此實時MOT系統經常完全放棄外觀線索，僅依賴運動預測和幾何關聯。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們介紹了Polycepta，一個以物體為中心的外觀狀態估計框架，將外觀建模重新定義為一個遞歸估計問題，而不是幀對幀的匹配任務。<lb/>Polycepta為每個被追蹤的物體構建並持續更新獨立的外觀狀態，使未來的外觀表示能夠從累積的觀察中進行估計。<lb/>Polycepta被鼓勵學習物體特定表示的外觀狀態構建，而不是通過提出的學習策略來記憶它們，從而實現對未見類別的外觀估計。<lb/>Polycepta的一個關鍵特性是，隨著物體狀態在推理過程中的演變，外觀估計的質量會提高。<lb/>雖然傳統的外觀描述符保持靜態或隨時間退化，但Polycepta會隨著額外觀察的累積而逐步改進外觀估計。<lb/>在KITTI、Waymo開放數據集和MOT17上的大量實驗顯示，當集成到追蹤-通過-檢測管道中時，身份切換的數量穩定減少，追蹤性能顯著提高。<lb/>Polycepta以90.57 Hz的速度運行，並在集成到RobMOT框架中時，在KITTI基準上實現了最先進的性能，達到92.27\%的MOTA。
2606.23604.author: Mohamed Nagy et.al.
2606.23604.authors: Mohamed Nagy, Naoufel Werghi, Jorge Dias, Majid Khonji
